ddls:
CREATE TABLE "sessions" (
  "session_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the video conferencing session */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the session starts */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the session ends */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user participating in the session */,
  "duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the session in seconds */,
  "session_name" TEXT /* Name or title of the session */,
  "number_of_participants" INTEGER /* Total number of participants in the session */,
  "is_recording" INTEGER /* Indicates if the session was recorded */,
  "recording_url" TEXT /* URL to the recorded session (if available) */,
  "session_type" TEXT /* Type of session (e.g., meeting, webinar, training) */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location where the session took place (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("session_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user in the organization (e.g., participant, admin) */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the user */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department or organization unit of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audio_metrics" (
  "audio_metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the audio metrics record */,
  "session_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the session associated with these metrics */,
  "audio_bytes_sent" INTEGER /* Total bytes of audio data sent during the session */,
  "audio_packets_sent" INTEGER /* Total packets of audio data sent during the session */,
  "audio_nack_count" INTEGER /* Count of NACKs received for audio packets */,
  "audio_bitrate" INTEGER /* Average bitrate of the audio data */,
  "audio_quality_score" REAL /* Quality score for the audio (e.g., MOS score) */,
  "audio_jitter" REAL /* Jitter experienced in the audio stream */,
  "audio_packet_loss" REAL /* Percentage of audio packets lost */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("audio_metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audio_metrics_session_id FOREIGN KEY ("session_id") REFERENCES sessions ("session_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "video_metrics" (
  "video_metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the video metrics record */,
  "session_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the session associated with these metrics */,
  "video_bytes_sent" INTEGER /* Total bytes of video data sent during the session */,
  "video_packets_sent" INTEGER /* Total packets of video data sent during the session */,
  "video_frames_encoded" INTEGER /* Total number of frames encoded */,
  "video_nack_count" INTEGER /* Count of NACKs received for video packets */,
  "video_fir_count" INTEGER /* Count of FIRs (Full Intra Request) sent */,
  "video_pli_count" INTEGER /* Count of PLIs (Picture Loss Indication) sent */,
  "video_qp_sum" INTEGER /* Sum of quality parameters (QP) for video encoding */,
  "video_bitrate" INTEGER /* Average bitrate of the video data */,
  "video_quality_score" REAL /* Quality score for the video */,
  "video_jitter" REAL /* Jitter experienced in the video stream */,
  "video_fec_used" INTEGER /* Indicates if forward error correction (FEC) was used */,
  "video_fec_packets_sent" INTEGER /* Number of FEC packets sent */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("video_metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_video_metrics_session_id FOREIGN KEY ("session_id") REFERENCES sessions ("session_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "performance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the performance metrics record */,
  "session_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the session linked to these metrics */,
  "average_audio_quality" REAL /* Average quality score for audio during the session */,
  "average_video_quality" REAL /* Average quality score for video during the session */,
  "total_packets_lost" INTEGER /* Total number of packets lost during the session */,
  "max_audio_bitrate" INTEGER /* Maximum audio bitrate during the session */,
  "max_video_bitrate" INTEGER /* Maximum video bitrate during the session */,
  "max_latency" REAL /* Maximum latency experienced during the session */,
  "max_jitter" REAL /* Maximum jitter experienced during the session */,
  "max_packet_loss" REAL /* Maximum packet loss percentage during the session */,
  "session_score" REAL /* Overall performance score for the session */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("performance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_session_id FOREIGN KEY ("session_id") REFERENCES sessions ("session_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the role */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the role was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the role was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "session_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "session_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the session linked to this log */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user associated with this activity */,
  "activity" TEXT /* Description of the activity performed by the user */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the activity occurred */,
  "log_type" TEXT /* Type of log entry (e.g., join, leave, mute, unmute) */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device used (e.g., computer, mobile, tablet) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the user's browser or app */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_session_logs_session_id FOREIGN KEY ("session_id") REFERENCES sessions ("session_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_session_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "network_conditions" (
  "condition_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each network condition record */,
  "session_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the session linked to these conditions */,
  "latency" REAL /* Latency experienced during the session (in ms) */,
  "jitter" REAL /* Jitter experienced during the session (in ms) */,
  "packet_loss" REAL /* Percentage of packet loss during the session */,
  "download_speed" REAL /* Download speed experienced during the session */,
  "upload_speed" REAL /* Upload speed experienced during the session */,
  "connection_type" TEXT /* Type of connection (e.g., Wi-Fi, Ethernet, cellular) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("condition_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_conditions_session_id FOREIGN KEY ("session_id") REFERENCES sessions ("session_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "service_status" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the service status record */,
  "service_name" TEXT /* Name of the service being monitored */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the service is currently active */,
  "last_checked" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last check performed on the service */,
  "service_version" TEXT /* Version of the service */,
  "last_restarted" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last restart */,
  "status_message" TEXT /* Detailed status message (e.g., 'Service is running normally') */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "session_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the session linked to this alert */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of the alert (e.g., performance issue, connectivity issue) */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Detailed message regarding the alert */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was generated */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the alert (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "resolved" INTEGER /* Indicates if the alert has been resolved */,
  "resolved_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who resolved the alert */,
  "resolution_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was resolved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_resolved_by FOREIGN KEY ("resolved_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_session_id FOREIGN KEY ("session_id") REFERENCES sessions ("session_id")
)
